clauses.statement: skills/vibepro-gate-evidence/SKILL.md documents the closure procedure for gate:evidence_adjudication and gate:judgment_dag_adjudication: vibepro adjudicate prepare (with --judgment for the judgment DAG) generates the request, an independent fresh-context subagent (not the implementing agent) is dispatched, and vibepro adjudicate record (with --judgment) records per-clause / per-item verdicts.

skills/vibepro-gate-evidence/SKILL.md documents the adjudication verdict vocabularies (demonstrated / not_demonstrated / not_verifiable_by_automation and judged_sound / judged_unsound / needs_human_judgment) and the human-closure procedure via vibepro decision record with source gate:evidence_adjudication:<clause-id> (judgment side gate:judgment_dag_adjudication:<item-id>) plus both reason and artifact.

skills/vibepro-gate-evidence/SKILL.md states that adjudication verdicts are head-bound and fail closed: a verdict without a recorded head_commit, or evaluated when the current HEAD is unknown, never counts as fresh.

skills/vibepro-gate-evidence/SKILL.md documents the scanner conclusiveness distinction: inconclusive (zero scanned targets is not evidence of a pass) versus not_applicable (out of scope), and explicitly forbids presenting an inconclusive result as a passing gate.
The Operating Order in skills/vibepro-workflow/SKILL.md includes an adjudication step that closes gate:evidence_adjudication and gate:judgment_dag_adjudication after pr prepare and before pr create.

skills/vibepro-workflow/SKILL.md documents the Release Surface Guard: vibepro guard check|install|status commands, recovery through pr prepare when a release surface is blocked, and VIBEPRO_GUARD_BYPASS bypasses recorded with a reason to .vibepro/guard/bypass-log.jsonl for audit.
node bin/vibepro.js skills lint . reports pass for all skills with zero errors and warnings after the documentation changes.
CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md remain byte-for-byte identical and unchanged by this story (cmp -s CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md exits 0 and neither file appears in the diff).

diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[tree finalized + committed] --> B[vibepro verify record]
  B --> C[Agent Review lifecycle]
  C --> D[vibepro adjudicate prepare / --judgment]
  D --> E[independent fresh-context subagent judges clauses and items]
  E --> F[vibepro adjudicate record / --judgment]
  F --> G[vibepro pr prepare]
  G --> H{ready_for_pr_create?}
  H -- yes --> I[vibepro pr create]
  H -- no --> B
  I --> J[Release Surface Guard allows release surfaces]
diagrams.rationale: Documented gate-closure workflow order that the updated Skills describe (Story AC-1, AC-5).

diagrams.mermaid:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> missing
  missing --> demonstrated: adjudicate record (fresh head_commit)
  missing --> not_demonstrated: adjudicate record
  missing --> not_verifiable_by_automation: adjudicate record
  not_demonstrated --> missing: new evidence + re-adjudication
  not_verifiable_by_automation --> human_closed: accepted decision record (reason + artifact)
  demonstrated --> missing: new commit (head-bound, fail closed)
  human_closed --> [*]
  demonstrated --> [*]
diagrams.rationale: Adjudication verdict lifecycle documented in the updated gate-evidence Skill (Story AC-2, AC-3): a clause starts missing, moves to a recorded verdict, and not_verifiable_by_automation becomes human_closed via an accepted decision record.
